82367437c261a52958e442dbc1202e3aaa22e684�8236743�Oussama Sekkat�osekkat@gmail.com�2026-03-23T19:04:39+01:00�Oussama Sekkat�osekkat@gmail.com�2026-03-23T19:04:39+01:00�821311835ede3e0472a43b6b50f20eebabfa0800�Wire end-to-end audio call: relay WebTransport + codec workers + SDK orchestration�Replace the stubbed connection handler with a working audio forwarding
pipeline so two browser tabs can join the same room and hear each other.

Relay (Rust):
- Add wtransport crate for HTTP/3 WebTransport server (replaces raw QUIC)
- Rewrite main.rs: wtransport endpoint on :4433, shared RoomRegistry +
  JoinTokenAuthority, dev-token HTTP server on :4434 with cert hash header
- Add handle_session() in transport/mod.rs: parse CBOR JOIN from control
  stream, verify HMAC token, register in room, forward datagrams, cleanup
- Update routing/mod.rs: Participant uses wtransport::Connection

Client (TypeScript):
- Encode worker: accumulate 128-sample AudioWorklet chunks into 960-sample
  Opus frames, encode via WebCodecs, serialize as AudioSlice wire format.
  Promise-chain serialization prevents overlapping WebCodecs flush() calls.
- Decode worker: deserialize AudioSlice, decode Opus via WebCodecs, post
  PCM to playback. Same promise-chain serialization for correctness.
- SDK join(): fetch dev token, create transport/encode/decode workers,
  wire message routing between them, capture mic via AudioWorklet (Blob URL),
  play received audio via AudioWorklet, pass serverCertificateHashes for
  self-signed certs (no Chrome flags needed), resume AudioContext.
- WebTransportClient: add serverCertificateHashes option passthrough
- Transport worker: pass serverCertificateHashes to WebTransportClient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

committer Oussama Sekkat <osekkat@gmail.com> 1774289079 +0100

    Wire end-to-end audio call: relay WebTransport + codec workers + SDK orchestration
    
    Replace the stubbed connection handler with a working audio forwarding
    pipeline so two browser tabs can join the same room and hear each other.
    
    Relay (Rust):
    - Add wtransport crate for HTTP/3 WebTransport server (replaces raw QUIC)
    - Rewrite main.rs: wtransport endpoint on :4433, shared RoomRegistry +
      JoinTokenAuthority, dev-token HTTP server on :4434 with cert hash header
    - Add handle_session() in transport/mod.rs: parse CBOR JOIN from control
      stream, verify HMAC token, register in room, forward datagrams, cleanup
    - Update routing/mod.rs: Participant uses wtransport::Connection
    
    Client (TypeScript):
    - Encode worker: accumulate 128-sample AudioWorklet chunks into 960-sample
      Opus frames, encode via WebCodecs, serialize as AudioSlice wire format.
      Promise-chain serialization prevents overlapping WebCodecs flush() calls.
    - Decode worker: deserialize AudioSlice, decode Opus via WebCodecs, post
      PCM to playback. Same promise-chain serialization for correctness.
    - SDK join(): fetch dev token, create transport/encode/decode workers,
      wire message routing between them, capture mic via AudioWorklet (Blob URL),
      play received audio via AudioWorklet, pass serverCertificateHashes for
      self-signed certs (no Chrome flags needed), resume AudioContext.
    - WebTransportClient: add serverCertificateHashes option passthrough
    - Transport worker: pass serverCertificateHashes to WebTransportClient
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+/// Tiny HTTP server on port 4434 serving dev join tokens.
+///
+/// `GET /dev-token?room=ROOM_ID` → returns signed join token as raw bytes.
+/// This is for development only — production uses proper token issuance.
+async fn dev_token_server(authority: Arc<auth::JoinTokenAuthority>, cert_hash: String) -> Result<()> {
+    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("[::]:4434").await?;
+    info!(addr = "[::]:4434", "dev token server listening (HTTP)");
+
+    loop {
+        let (mut stream, _addr) = listener.accept().await?;
+        let authority = authority.clone();
+        let cert_hash = cert_hash.clone();
+
+        tokio::spawn(async move {
+            let mut buf_reader =
+                tokio::io::BufReader::new(&mut stream);
+            let mut request_line = String::new();
+            if buf_reader.read_line(&mut request_line).await.is_err() {
+                return;
+            }
+
+            // Parse room from query string: GET /dev-token?room=ROOM_ID HTTP/1.1
+            let room_id = request_line
+                .split_whitespace()
+                .nth(1)
+                .and_then(|path| path.split("room=").nth(1))
+                .map(|r| r.split('&').next().unwrap_or(r))
+                .unwrap_or("default")
+                .to_string();
+
+            // Simple percent-decode for dev use (no external dep needed)
+            let room_id = room_id.replace("%20", " ");
+
+            // Drain remaining headers
+            loop {
+                let mut line = String::new();
+                if buf_reader.read_line(&mut line).await.is_err() || line.trim().is_empty() {
+                    break;
+                }
+            }
+
+            let now = SystemTime::now()
+                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
+                .unwrap()
+                .as_secs();
+
+            let grant = auth::RoomGrant {
+                room_id: room_id.clone(),
+                auth_subject: format!("dev:user-{}", now % 10000),
+                participant_kind: auth::ParticipantKind::Human,
+                permissions: auth::Permissions::full(),
+                expires_at: now + 3600,
+                tenant_id: None,
+            };
+
+            match authority.sign(&grant) {
+                Ok(token) => {
+                    let headers = format!(
+                        "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, OPTIONS\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Headers: *\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Expose-Headers: X-Cert-Hash\r\n\
+                         Content-Type: application/octet-stream\r\n\
+                         X-Cert-Hash: {}\r\n\
+                         Content-Length: {}\r\n\
+                         \r\n",
+                        cert_hash,
+                        token.bytes.len()
+                    );
+                    let _ = buf_reader.get_mut().write_all(headers.as_bytes()).await;
+                    let _ = buf_reader.get_mut().write_all(&token.bytes).await;
+                }
+                Err(e) => {
+                    let msg = format!("token signing failed: {e}");
+                    let resp = format!(
+                        "HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *\r\n\
+                         Content-Length: {}\r\n\
+                         \r\n\
+                         {msg}",
+                        msg.len()
+                    );
+                    let _ = buf_reader.get_mut().write_all(resp.as_bytes()).await;
+                }
+            }
+        });
+    }
 }

+/// Monotonically incrementing participant ID counter.

+fn assigner() -> &'static SessionAssigner {

+// ─── Wire types for CBOR control messages from browser ──────────────
+
+/// Outer framing: matches `encodeSessionMessage()` from control.ts.
+/// The browser may send additional fields (e.g. `extensions`) — we ignore them.
+#[derive(Deserialize, Debug)]
+struct WireSessionMessage {
+    #[serde(rename = "protocolVersion")]
+    protocol_version: u32,
+    #[serde(rename = "minSupportedVersion")]
+    #[allow(dead_code)]
+    min_supported_version: u32,
+    msg: ciborium::Value,
+}
+
+/// Parsed JOIN message fields extracted from the `msg` CBOR Value.
+#[derive(Debug)]
+struct ParsedJoin {
+    auth: WireJoinAuth,
+    requested_display_name: String,
+}
+
+/// Parse a JOIN control message from a CBOR Value map.
+/// Manually extracts known fields and ignores extras like `capabilities`.
+fn parse_join_message(msg: &ciborium::Value) -> Result<ParsedJoin> {
+    let map = match msg {
+        ciborium::Value::Map(m) => m,
+        _ => return Err(anyhow!("expected CBOR map for control message")),
+    };
+
+    // Helper to find a text field in the CBOR map
+    let find = |key: &str| -> Option<&ciborium::Value> {
+        map.iter()
+            .find(|(k, _)| matches!(k, ciborium::Value::Text(t) if t == key))
+            .map(|(_, v)| v)
+    };
+
+    // Verify this is a "join" message
+    let msg_type = find("type")
+        .and_then(|v| match v { ciborium::Value::Text(t) => Some(t.as_str()), _ => None })
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing or invalid 'type' field in control message"))?;
+
+    if msg_type != "join" {
+        return Err(anyhow!("expected 'join' message, got '{msg_type}'"));
+    }
+
+    // Extract auth.joinToken
+    let auth_map = find("auth")
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing 'auth' field in join message"))?;
+
+    let join_token = match auth_map {
+        ciborium::Value::Map(am) => {
+            am.iter()
+                .find(|(k, _)| matches!(k, ciborium::Value::Text(t) if t == "joinToken"))
+                .map(|(_, v)| v.clone())
+                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing 'joinToken' in auth"))?
+        }
+        _ => return Err(anyhow!("'auth' is not a map")),
+    };
+
+    // Extract requestedDisplayName
+    let display_name = find("requestedDisplayName")
+        .and_then(|v| match v { ciborium::Value::Text(t) => Some(t.clone()), _ => None })
+        .unwrap_or_default();
+
+    Ok(ParsedJoin {
+        auth: WireJoinAuth { join_token },
+        requested_display_name: display_name,
+    })
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug)]
+struct WireJoinAuth {
+    join_token: ciborium::Value,
+}
+
+impl WireJoinAuth {
+    /// Extract raw bytes from the joinToken field.
+    /// CBOR may encode this as Bytes or as an Array of integers.
+    fn token_bytes(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
+        match &self.join_token {
+            ciborium::Value::Bytes(b) => Ok(b.clone()),
+            ciborium::Value::Array(arr) => {
+                let mut bytes = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());
+                for v in arr {
+                    match v {
+                        ciborium::Value::Integer(i) => {
+                            let n: i128 = (*i).into();
+                            if !(0..=255).contains(&n) {
+                                return Err(anyhow!("token byte out of range: {n}"));
+                            }
+                            bytes.push(n as u8);
+                        }
+                        _ => return Err(anyhow!("unexpected token array element: {v:?}")),
+                    }
+                }
+                Ok(bytes)
+            }
+            other => Err(anyhow!("unexpected joinToken type: {other:?}")),
+        }
+    }
+}

+/// Handle an authenticated WebTransport session: parse JOIN, register
+/// participant, forward datagrams, and clean up on disconnect.
+pub async fn handle_session(
+    connection: wtransport::Connection,
+    registry: Arc<RoomRegistry>,
+    authority: Arc<JoinTokenAuthority>,
+) -> Result<()> {
+    let remote = connection.remote_address();
+
+    // ── Step 1: Accept the control stream (Channel 2) ────────────────
+    let (mut send_stream, mut recv_stream) = connection.accept_bi().await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to accept control stream: {e}"))?;
+
+    // ── Step 2: Read the JOIN message ────────────────────────────────
+    // Read up to 4KB of CBOR (more than enough for a JOIN message)
+    let mut buf = vec![0u8; 4096];
+    let n = recv_stream.read(&mut buf).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to read control stream: {e}"))?
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("control stream closed before JOIN"))?;
+
+    let wire_msg: WireSessionMessage = ciborium::from_reader(&buf[..n])
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to decode JOIN message: {e}"))?;
+
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        protocol_version = wire_msg.protocol_version,
+        "received control message"
+    );
+
+    let parsed = parse_join_message(&wire_msg.msg)?;
+
+    // ── Step 3: Verify join token ────────────────────────────────────
+    let token_bytes = parsed.auth.token_bytes()?;
+    let grant = authority.verify(&token_bytes)
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("join token verification failed: {e}"))?;
+
+    let room_id = grant.room_id.clone();
+    let instance_id = assigner().new_participant_id();
+    let session_id = assigner().new_session_id();
+    let display_name = if parsed.requested_display_name.is_empty() {
+        grant.auth_subject.clone()
+    } else {
+        parsed.requested_display_name
+    };
+
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        display_name = %display_name,
+        "join verified"
+    );
+
+    // ── Step 4: Register in room ─────────────────────────────────────
+    let participant = Participant {
+        instance_id: instance_id.clone(),
+        auth_subject: grant.auth_subject.clone(),
+        display_name: display_name.clone(),
+        connection: connection.clone(),
+    };
+
+    registry.join_room(&room_id, participant).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to join room: {e}"))?;
+
+    // ── Step 5: Send JOIN_ACCEPT ─────────────────────────────────────
+    let accept_msg = ciborium::Value::Map(vec![
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("protocolVersion".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Integer(0.into()),
+        ),
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("minSupportedVersion".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Integer(0.into()),
+        ),
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("msg".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Map(vec![
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("type".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("join_accept".to_string()),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("authSubject".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(grant.auth_subject),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("participantInstanceId".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(instance_id.clone()),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("sessionId".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(session_id),
+                ),
+            ]),
+        ),
+    ]);
+
+    let mut accept_bytes = Vec::new();
+    ciborium::into_writer(&accept_msg, &mut accept_bytes)
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to encode JOIN_ACCEPT: {e}"))?;
+
+    use tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt;
+    send_stream.write_all(&accept_bytes).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to send JOIN_ACCEPT: {e}"))?;
+
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        "session established, entering forwarding loop"
+    );
+
+    // ── Step 6: Audio forwarding loop ────────────────────────────────
+    loop {
+        tokio::select! {
+            datagram = connection.receive_datagram() => {
+                match datagram {
+                    Ok(data) => {
+                        registry.forward_audio(&room_id, &instance_id, &data).await;
+                    }
+                    Err(e) => {
+                        info!(
+                            remote = %remote,
+                            room = %room_id,
+                            participant = %instance_id,
+                            error = %e,
+                            "datagram receive ended"
+                        );
+                        break;
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+            error = connection.closed() => {
+                info!(
+                    remote = %remote,
+                    room = %room_id,
+                    participant = %instance_id,
+                    error = %error,
+                    "connection closed"
+                );
+                break;
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    // ── Step 7: Clean up ─────────────────────────────────────────────
+    registry.leave_room(&room_id, &instance_id).await;
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        "session cleaned up"
+    );
+
+    Ok(())
+}

+/// Compute the base64-encoded SHA-256 hash of the self-signed certificate
+/// for use with Chrome's `--ignore-certificate-errors-spki-list` flag.
+pub fn compute_cert_hash(identity: &wtransport::Identity) -> String {
+    let cert_chain = identity.certificate_chain();
+    let certs = cert_chain.as_slice();
+    if certs.is_empty() {
+        return "NO_CERT".to_string();
+    }
+    let hash = certs[0].hash();
+    base64_encode(hash.as_ref())
+}
+
+/// Simple base64 encoding (no external dep needed for this small use).
+fn base64_encode(data: &[u8]) -> String {
+    const CHARS: &[u8] = b"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/";
+    let mut result = String::with_capacity((data.len() + 2) / 3 * 4);
+    for chunk in data.chunks(3) {
+        let b0 = chunk[0] as u32;
+        let b1 = chunk.get(1).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;
+        let b2 = chunk.get(2).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;
+        let triple = (b0 << 16) | (b1 << 8) | b2;
+        result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 18) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 12) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        if chunk.len() > 1 {
+            result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 6) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        } else {
+            result.push('=');
+        }
+        if chunk.len() > 2 {
+            result.push(CHARS[(triple & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        } else {
+            result.push('=');
+        }
+    }
+    result
+}

+// ─── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/** Send an init message to a worker and wait for the "ready" response. */
+function workerReady(
+  worker: Worker,
+  initMsg: Record<string, unknown>,
+): Promise<void> {
+  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
+    const timeout = setTimeout(
+      () => reject(new Error("Worker init timeout")),
+      5000,
+    );
+    const handler = (e: MessageEvent) => {
+      if (e.data.type === "ready") {
+        clearTimeout(timeout);
+        worker.removeEventListener("message", handler);
+        resolve();
+      }
+    };
+    worker.addEventListener("message", handler);
+    worker.postMessage(initMsg);
+  });
+}

author Oussama Sekkat <osekkat@gmail.com> 1774289079 +0100
committer Oussama Sekkat <osekkat@gmail.com> 1774289079 +0100

    Wire end-to-end audio call: relay WebTransport + codec workers + SDK orchestration
    
    Replace the stubbed connection handler with a working audio forwarding
    pipeline so two browser tabs can join the same room and hear each other.
    
    Relay (Rust):
    - Add wtransport crate for HTTP/3 WebTransport server (replaces raw QUIC)
    - Rewrite main.rs: wtransport endpoint on :4433, shared RoomRegistry +
      JoinTokenAuthority, dev-token HTTP server on :4434 with cert hash header
    - Add handle_session() in transport/mod.rs: parse CBOR JOIN from control
      stream, verify HMAC token, register in room, forward datagrams, cleanup
    - Update routing/mod.rs: Participant uses wtransport::Connection
    
    Client (TypeScript):
    - Encode worker: accumulate 128-sample AudioWorklet chunks into 960-sample
      Opus frames, encode via WebCodecs, serialize as AudioSlice wire format.
      Promise-chain serialization prevents overlapping WebCodecs flush() calls.
    - Decode worker: deserialize AudioSlice, decode Opus via WebCodecs, post
      PCM to playback. Same promise-chain serialization for correctness.
    - SDK join(): fetch dev token, create transport/encode/decode workers,
      wire message routing between them, capture mic via AudioWorklet (Blob URL),
      play received audio via AudioWorklet, pass serverCertificateHashes for
      self-signed certs (no Chrome flags needed), resume AudioContext.
    - WebTransportClient: add serverCertificateHashes option passthrough
    - Transport worker: pass serverCertificateHashes to WebTransportClient
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+/// Tiny HTTP server on port 4434 serving dev join tokens.
+///
+/// `GET /dev-token?room=ROOM_ID` → returns signed join token as raw bytes.
+/// This is for development only — production uses proper token issuance.
+async fn dev_token_server(authority: Arc<auth::JoinTokenAuthority>, cert_hash: String) -> Result<()> {
+    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("[::]:4434").await?;
+    info!(addr = "[::]:4434", "dev token server listening (HTTP)");
+
+    loop {
+        let (mut stream, _addr) = listener.accept().await?;
+        let authority = authority.clone();
+        let cert_hash = cert_hash.clone();
+
+        tokio::spawn(async move {
+            let mut buf_reader =
+                tokio::io::BufReader::new(&mut stream);
+            let mut request_line = String::new();
+            if buf_reader.read_line(&mut request_line).await.is_err() {
+                return;
+            }
+
+            // Parse room from query string: GET /dev-token?room=ROOM_ID HTTP/1.1
+            let room_id = request_line
+                .split_whitespace()
+                .nth(1)
+                .and_then(|path| path.split("room=").nth(1))
+                .map(|r| r.split('&').next().unwrap_or(r))
+                .unwrap_or("default")
+                .to_string();
+
+            // Simple percent-decode for dev use (no external dep needed)
+            let room_id = room_id.replace("%20", " ");
+
+            // Drain remaining headers
+            loop {
+                let mut line = String::new();
+                if buf_reader.read_line(&mut line).await.is_err() || line.trim().is_empty() {
+                    break;
+                }
+            }
+
+            let now = SystemTime::now()
+                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
+                .unwrap()
+                .as_secs();
+
+            let grant = auth::RoomGrant {
+                room_id: room_id.clone(),
+                auth_subject: format!("dev:user-{}", now % 10000),
+                participant_kind: auth::ParticipantKind::Human,
+                permissions: auth::Permissions::full(),
+                expires_at: now + 3600,
+                tenant_id: None,
+            };

+            match authority.sign(&grant) {
+                Ok(token) => {
+                    let headers = format!(
+                        "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, OPTIONS\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Headers: *\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Expose-Headers: X-Cert-Hash\r\n\
+                         Content-Type: application/octet-stream\r\n\
+                         X-Cert-Hash: {}\r\n\
+                         Content-Length: {}\r\n\

+                         Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *\r\n\
+                         Content-Length: {}\r\n\
+                         \r\n\
+                         {msg}",
+                        msg.len()
+                    );
+                    let _ = buf_reader.get_mut().write_all(resp.as_bytes()).await;
+                }
+            }
+        });
+    }

+// ─── Wire types for CBOR control messages from browser ──────────────
+
+/// Outer framing: matches `encodeSessionMessage()` from control.ts.
+/// The browser may send additional fields (e.g. `extensions`) — we ignore them.
+#[derive(Deserialize, Debug)]
+struct WireSessionMessage {
+    #[serde(rename = "protocolVersion")]
+    protocol_version: u32,
+    #[serde(rename = "minSupportedVersion")]
+    #[allow(dead_code)]
+    min_supported_version: u32,
+    msg: ciborium::Value,
+}
+
+/// Parsed JOIN message fields extracted from the `msg` CBOR Value.
+#[derive(Debug)]
+struct ParsedJoin {
+    auth: WireJoinAuth,
+    requested_display_name: String,
+}
+
+/// Parse a JOIN control message from a CBOR Value map.
+/// Manually extracts known fields and ignores extras like `capabilities`.
+fn parse_join_message(msg: &ciborium::Value) -> Result<ParsedJoin> {
+    let map = match msg {
+        ciborium::Value::Map(m) => m,
+        _ => return Err(anyhow!("expected CBOR map for control message")),
+    };
+
+    // Helper to find a text field in the CBOR map
+    let find = |key: &str| -> Option<&ciborium::Value> {
+        map.iter()
+            .find(|(k, _)| matches!(k, ciborium::Value::Text(t) if t == key))
+            .map(|(_, v)| v)
+    };
+
+    // Verify this is a "join" message
+    let msg_type = find("type")
+        .and_then(|v| match v { ciborium::Value::Text(t) => Some(t.as_str()), _ => None })
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing or invalid 'type' field in control message"))?;
+
+    if msg_type != "join" {
+        return Err(anyhow!("expected 'join' message, got '{msg_type}'"));
+    }
+
+    // Extract auth.joinToken
+    let auth_map = find("auth")
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing 'auth' field in join message"))?;
+
+    let join_token = match auth_map {
+        ciborium::Value::Map(am) => {
+            am.iter()
+                .find(|(k, _)| matches!(k, ciborium::Value::Text(t) if t == "joinToken"))
+                .map(|(_, v)| v.clone())
+                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing 'joinToken' in auth"))?
+        }
+        _ => return Err(anyhow!("'auth' is not a map")),
+    };
+
+    // Extract requestedDisplayName
+    let display_name = find("requestedDisplayName")
+        .and_then(|v| match v { ciborium::Value::Text(t) => Some(t.clone()), _ => None })
+        .unwrap_or_default();
+
+    Ok(ParsedJoin {
+        auth: WireJoinAuth { join_token },
+        requested_display_name: display_name,
+    })
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug)]
+struct WireJoinAuth {
+    join_token: ciborium::Value,
+}
+
+impl WireJoinAuth {
+    /// Extract raw bytes from the joinToken field.
+    /// CBOR may encode this as Bytes or as an Array of integers.
+    fn token_bytes(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
+        match &self.join_token {
+            ciborium::Value::Bytes(b) => Ok(b.clone()),
+            ciborium::Value::Array(arr) => {
+                let mut bytes = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());
+                for v in arr {
+                    match v {
+                        ciborium::Value::Integer(i) => {
+                            let n: i128 = (*i).into();
+                            if !(0..=255).contains(&n) {
+                                return Err(anyhow!("token byte out of range: {n}"));
+                            }
+                            bytes.push(n as u8);
+                        }
+                        _ => return Err(anyhow!("unexpected token array element: {v:?}")),
+                    }
+                }
+                Ok(bytes)
+            }
+            other => Err(anyhow!("unexpected joinToken type: {other:?}")),
+        }
+    }
+}

+/// Handle an authenticated WebTransport session: parse JOIN, register
+/// participant, forward datagrams, and clean up on disconnect.
+pub async fn handle_session(
+    connection: wtransport::Connection,
+    registry: Arc<RoomRegistry>,
+    authority: Arc<JoinTokenAuthority>,
+) -> Result<()> {
+    let remote = connection.remote_address();
+
+    // ── Step 1: Accept the control stream (Channel 2) ────────────────
+    let (mut send_stream, mut recv_stream) = connection.accept_bi().await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to accept control stream: {e}"))?;
+
+    // ── Step 2: Read the JOIN message ────────────────────────────────
+    // Read up to 4KB of CBOR (more than enough for a JOIN message)
+    let mut buf = vec![0u8; 4096];
+    let n = recv_stream.read(&mut buf).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to read control stream: {e}"))?
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("control stream closed before JOIN"))?;
+
+    let wire_msg: WireSessionMessage = ciborium::from_reader(&buf[..n])
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to decode JOIN message: {e}"))?;
+
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        protocol_version = wire_msg.protocol_version,
+        "received control message"
+    );
+
+    let parsed = parse_join_message(&wire_msg.msg)?;
+
+    // ── Step 3: Verify join token ────────────────────────────────────
+    let token_bytes = parsed.auth.token_bytes()?;
+    let grant = authority.verify(&token_bytes)
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("join token verification failed: {e}"))?;
+
+    let room_id = grant.room_id.clone();
+    let instance_id = assigner().new_participant_id();
+    let session_id = assigner().new_session_id();
+    let display_name = if parsed.requested_display_name.is_empty() {
+        grant.auth_subject.clone()
+    } else {
+        parsed.requested_display_name
+    };
+
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        display_name = %display_name,
+        "join verified"
+    );
+
+    // ── Step 4: Register in room ─────────────────────────────────────
+    let participant = Participant {
+        instance_id: instance_id.clone(),
+        auth_subject: grant.auth_subject.clone(),
+        display_name: display_name.clone(),
+        connection: connection.clone(),
+    };
+
+    registry.join_room(&room_id, participant).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to join room: {e}"))?;
+
+    // ── Step 5: Send JOIN_ACCEPT ─────────────────────────────────────
+    let accept_msg = ciborium::Value::Map(vec![
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("protocolVersion".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Integer(0.into()),
+        ),
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("minSupportedVersion".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Integer(0.into()),
+        ),
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("msg".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Map(vec![
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("type".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("join_accept".to_string()),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("authSubject".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(grant.auth_subject),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("participantInstanceId".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(instance_id.clone()),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("sessionId".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(session_id),
+                ),
+            ]),
+        ),
+    ]);
+
+    let mut accept_bytes = Vec::new();
+    ciborium::into_writer(&accept_msg, &mut accept_bytes)
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to encode JOIN_ACCEPT: {e}"))?;
+
+    use tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt;
+    send_stream.write_all(&accept_bytes).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to send JOIN_ACCEPT: {e}"))?;
+
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        "session established, entering forwarding loop"
+    );
+
+    // ── Step 6: Audio forwarding loop ────────────────────────────────
+    loop {
+        tokio::select! {
+            datagram = connection.receive_datagram() => {
+                match datagram {
+                    Ok(data) => {
+                        registry.forward_audio(&room_id, &instance_id, &data).await;
+                    }
+                    Err(e) => {
+                        info!(
+                            remote = %remote,
+                            room = %room_id,
+                            participant = %instance_id,
+                            error = %e,
+                            "datagram receive ended"
+                        );
+                        break;
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+            error = connection.closed() => {
+                info!(
+                    remote = %remote,
+                    room = %room_id,
+                    participant = %instance_id,
+                    error = %error,
+                    "connection closed"
+                );
+                break;
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    // ── Step 7: Clean up ─────────────────────────────────────────────
+    registry.leave_room(&room_id, &instance_id).await;
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        "session cleaned up"
+    );
+
+    Ok(())
+}
+
+/// Compute the base64-encoded SHA-256 hash of the self-signed certificate
+/// for use with Chrome's `--ignore-certificate-errors-spki-list` flag.
+pub fn compute_cert_hash(identity: &wtransport::Identity) -> String {
+    let cert_chain = identity.certificate_chain();
+    let certs = cert_chain.as_slice();
+    if certs.is_empty() {
+        return "NO_CERT".to_string();
+    }
+    let hash = certs[0].hash();
+    base64_encode(hash.as_ref())
+}
+
+/// Simple base64 encoding (no external dep needed for this small use).
+fn base64_encode(data: &[u8]) -> String {
+    const CHARS: &[u8] = b"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/";
+    let mut result = String::with_capacity((data.len() + 2) / 3 * 4);
+    for chunk in data.chunks(3) {
+        let b0 = chunk[0] as u32;
+        let b1 = chunk.get(1).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;
+        let b2 = chunk.get(2).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;
+        let triple = (b0 << 16) | (b1 << 8) | b2;
+        result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 18) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 12) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        if chunk.len() > 1 {
+            result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 6) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        } else {
+            result.push('=');
+        }
+        if chunk.len() > 2 {
+            result.push(CHARS[(triple & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        } else {
+            result.push('=');
+        }
+    }
+    result
+}

+function workerReady(
+  worker: Worker,
+  initMsg: Record<string, unknown>,
+): Promise<void> {
+  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
+    const timeout = setTimeout(
+      () => reject(new Error("Worker init timeout")),
+      5000,
+    );
+    const handler = (e: MessageEvent) => {
+      if (e.data.type === "ready") {
+        clearTimeout(timeout);
+        worker.removeEventListener("message", handler);
+        resolve();
+      }
+    };
+    worker.addEventListener("message", handler);
+    worker.postMessage(initMsg);
+  });
+}

commits.subject: Wire end-to-end audio call: relay WebTransport + codec workers + SDK orchestration
commits.body:
Replace the stubbed connection handler with a working audio forwarding
pipeline so two browser tabs can join the same room and hear each other.

Relay (Rust):
- Add wtransport crate for HTTP/3 WebTransport server (replaces raw QUIC)
- Rewrite main.rs: wtransport endpoint on :4433, shared RoomRegistry +
  JoinTokenAuthority, dev-token HTTP server on :4434 with cert hash header
- Add handle_session() in transport/mod.rs: parse CBOR JOIN from control
  stream, verify HMAC token, register in room, forward datagrams, cleanup
- Update routing/mod.rs: Participant uses wtransport::Connection

Client (TypeScript):
- Encode worker: accumulate 128-sample AudioWorklet chunks into 960-sample
  Opus frames, encode via WebCodecs, serialize as AudioSlice wire format.
  Promise-chain serialization prevents overlapping WebCodecs flush() calls.
- Decode worker: deserialize AudioSlice, decode Opus via WebCodecs, post
  PCM to playback. Same promise-chain serialization for correctness.
- SDK join(): fetch dev token, create transport/encode/decode workers,
  wire message routing between them, capture mic via AudioWorklet (Blob URL),
  play received audio via AudioWorklet, pass serverCertificateHashes for
  self-signed certs (no Chrome flags needed), resume AudioContext.
- WebTransportClient: add serverCertificateHashes option passthrough
- Transport worker: pass serverCertificateHashes to WebTransportClient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.subject: Add web app entry point (index.html, main.tsx, App.tsx)
commits.body:
The Vite config, React components, hooks, and SDK all existed but the
HTML/React mount shell was never created, so `npm run dev` served a 404.
This wires up a pre-join screen and the ConferenceView layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+/// Tiny HTTP server on port 4434 serving dev join tokens.
+///
+/// `GET /dev-token?room=ROOM_ID` → returns signed join token as raw bytes.
+/// This is for development only — production uses proper token issuance.
+async fn dev_token_server(authority: Arc<auth::JoinTokenAuthority>, cert_hash: String) -> Result<()> {
+    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("[::]:4434").await?;
+    info!(addr = "[::]:4434", "dev token server listening (HTTP)");
+
+    loop {
+        let (mut stream, _addr) = listener.accept().await?;
+        let authority = authority.clone();
+        let cert_hash = cert_hash.clone();
+
+        tokio::spawn(async move {
+            let mut buf_reader =
+                tokio::io::BufReader::new(&mut stream);
+            let mut request_line = String::new();
+            if buf_reader.read_line(&mut request_line).await.is_err() {
+                return;
+            }
+
+            // Parse room from query string: GET /dev-token?room=ROOM_ID HTTP/1.1
+            let room_id = request_line
+                .split_whitespace()
+                .nth(1)
+                .and_then(|path| path.split("room=").nth(1))
+                .map(|r| r.split('&').next().unwrap_or(r))
+                .unwrap_or("default")
+                .to_string();
+
+            // Simple percent-decode for dev use (no external dep needed)
+            let room_id = room_id.replace("%20", " ");
+
+            // Drain remaining headers
+            loop {
+                let mut line = String::new();
+                if buf_reader.read_line(&mut line).await.is_err() || line.trim().is_empty() {
+                    break;
+                }
+            }
+
+            let now = SystemTime::now()
+                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
+                .unwrap()
+                .as_secs();
+
+            let grant = auth::RoomGrant {
+                room_id: room_id.clone(),
+                auth_subject: format!("dev:user-{}", now % 10000),
+                participant_kind: auth::ParticipantKind::Human,
+                permissions: auth::Permissions::full(),
+                expires_at: now + 3600,
+                tenant_id: None,
+            };
+
+            match authority.sign(&grant) {
+                Ok(token) => {
+                    let headers = format!(
+                        "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, OPTIONS\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Headers: *\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Expose-Headers: X-Cert-Hash\r\n\
+                         Content-Type: application/octet-stream\r\n\
+                         X-Cert-Hash: {}\r\n\
+                         Content-Length: {}\r\n\
+                         \r\n",
+                        cert_hash,
+                        token.bytes.len()
+                    );
+                    let _ = buf_reader.get_mut().write_all(headers.as_bytes()).await;
+                    let _ = buf_reader.get_mut().write_all(&token.bytes).await;
+                }
+                Err(e) => {
+                    let msg = format!("token signing failed: {e}");
+                    let resp = format!(
+                        "HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\r\n\
+                         Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *\r\n\
+                         Content-Length: {}\r\n\
+                         \r\n\
+                         {msg}",
+                        msg.len()
+                    );
+                    let _ = buf_reader.get_mut().write_all(resp.as_bytes()).await;
+                }
+            }
+        });
+    }
 }

+// ─── Wire types for CBOR control messages from browser ──────────────
+
+/// Outer framing: matches `encodeSessionMessage()` from control.ts.
+/// The browser may send additional fields (e.g. `extensions`) — we ignore them.
+#[derive(Deserialize, Debug)]
+struct WireSessionMessage {
+    #[serde(rename = "protocolVersion")]
+    protocol_version: u32,
+    #[serde(rename = "minSupportedVersion")]

+    min_supported_version: u32,

+/// Parsed JOIN message fields extracted from the `msg` CBOR Value.
+#[derive(Debug)]
+struct ParsedJoin {
+    auth: WireJoinAuth,
+    requested_display_name: String,

+
+/// Parse a JOIN control message from a CBOR Value map.
+/// Manually extracts known fields and ignores extras like `capabilities`.
+fn parse_join_message(msg: &ciborium::Value) -> Result<ParsedJoin> {
+    let map = match msg {
+        ciborium::Value::Map(m) => m,
+        _ => return Err(anyhow!("expected CBOR map for control message")),
+    };
+
+    // Helper to find a text field in the CBOR map
+    let find = |key: &str| -> Option<&ciborium::Value> {
+        map.iter()
+            .find(|(k, _)| matches!(k, ciborium::Value::Text(t) if t == key))
+            .map(|(_, v)| v)
+    };
+
+    // Verify this is a "join" message
+    let msg_type = find("type")
+        .and_then(|v| match v { ciborium::Value::Text(t) => Some(t.as_str()), _ => None })
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing or invalid 'type' field in control message"))?;
+
+    if msg_type != "join" {
+        return Err(anyhow!("expected 'join' message, got '{msg_type}'"));
+    }
+
+    // Extract auth.joinToken
+    let auth_map = find("auth")
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing 'auth' field in join message"))?;
+
+    let join_token = match auth_map {
+        ciborium::Value::Map(am) => {
+            am.iter()
+                .find(|(k, _)| matches!(k, ciborium::Value::Text(t) if t == "joinToken"))
+                .map(|(_, v)| v.clone())
+                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("missing 'joinToken' in auth"))?
+        }
+        _ => return Err(anyhow!("'auth' is not a map")),
+    };
+
+    // Extract requestedDisplayName
+    let display_name = find("requestedDisplayName")
+        .and_then(|v| match v { ciborium::Value::Text(t) => Some(t.clone()), _ => None })
+        .unwrap_or_default();
+
+    Ok(ParsedJoin {
+        auth: WireJoinAuth { join_token },
+        requested_display_name: display_name,
+    })

+#[derive(Debug)]

+    join_token: ciborium::Value,

+
+impl WireJoinAuth {
+    /// Extract raw bytes from the joinToken field.
+    /// CBOR may encode this as Bytes or as an Array of integers.
+    fn token_bytes(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
+        match &self.join_token {
+            ciborium::Value::Bytes(b) => Ok(b.clone()),
+            ciborium::Value::Array(arr) => {
+                let mut bytes = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());
+                for v in arr {
+                    match v {
+                        ciborium::Value::Integer(i) => {
+                            let n: i128 = (*i).into();
+                            if !(0..=255).contains(&n) {
+                                return Err(anyhow!("token byte out of range: {n}"));
+                            }
+                            bytes.push(n as u8);
+                        }
+                        _ => return Err(anyhow!("unexpected token array element: {v:?}")),
+                    }
+                }
+                Ok(bytes)
+            }
+            other => Err(anyhow!("unexpected joinToken type: {other:?}")),
+        }
+    }

+        instance_id: instance_id.clone(),
+        auth_subject: grant.auth_subject.clone(),
+        display_name: display_name.clone(),
+        connection: connection.clone(),
+    };
+
+    registry.join_room(&room_id, participant).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to join room: {e}"))?;
+
+    // ── Step 5: Send JOIN_ACCEPT ─────────────────────────────────────
+    let accept_msg = ciborium::Value::Map(vec![
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("protocolVersion".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Integer(0.into()),
+        ),
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("minSupportedVersion".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Integer(0.into()),
+        ),
+        (
+            ciborium::Value::Text("msg".to_string()),
+            ciborium::Value::Map(vec![
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("type".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("join_accept".to_string()),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("authSubject".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(grant.auth_subject),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("participantInstanceId".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(instance_id.clone()),
+                ),
+                (
+                    ciborium::Value::Text("sessionId".to_string()),
+                    ciborium::Value::Text(session_id),
+                ),
+            ]),
+        ),
+    ]);
+
+    let mut accept_bytes = Vec::new();
+    ciborium::into_writer(&accept_msg, &mut accept_bytes)
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to encode JOIN_ACCEPT: {e}"))?;
+
+    use tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt;
+    send_stream.write_all(&accept_bytes).await
+        .map_err(|e| anyhow!("failed to send JOIN_ACCEPT: {e}"))?;
+
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        "session established, entering forwarding loop"
+    );
+
+    // ── Step 6: Audio forwarding loop ────────────────────────────────
+    loop {
+        tokio::select! {
+            datagram = connection.receive_datagram() => {
+                match datagram {
+                    Ok(data) => {
+                        registry.forward_audio(&room_id, &instance_id, &data).await;
+                    }
+                    Err(e) => {
+                        info!(
+                            remote = %remote,
+                            room = %room_id,
+                            participant = %instance_id,
+                            error = %e,
+                            "datagram receive ended"
+                        );
+                        break;
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+            error = connection.closed() => {
+                info!(
+                    remote = %remote,
+                    room = %room_id,
+                    participant = %instance_id,
+                    error = %error,
+                    "connection closed"
+                );
+                break;
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    // ── Step 7: Clean up ─────────────────────────────────────────────
+    registry.leave_room(&room_id, &instance_id).await;
+    info!(
+        remote = %remote,
+        room = %room_id,
+        participant = %instance_id,
+        "session cleaned up"
+    );
+
+    Ok(())
+}

+/// Compute the base64-encoded SHA-256 hash of the self-signed certificate
+/// for use with Chrome's `--ignore-certificate-errors-spki-list` flag.
+pub fn compute_cert_hash(identity: &wtransport::Identity) -> String {
+    let cert_chain = identity.certificate_chain();
+    let certs = cert_chain.as_slice();
+    if certs.is_empty() {
+        return "NO_CERT".to_string();
+    }
+    let hash = certs[0].hash();
+    base64_encode(hash.as_ref())
+}
+
+/// Simple base64 encoding (no external dep needed for this small use).
+fn base64_encode(data: &[u8]) -> String {
+    const CHARS: &[u8] = b"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/";
+    let mut result = String::with_capacity((data.len() + 2) / 3 * 4);
+    for chunk in data.chunks(3) {
+        let b0 = chunk[0] as u32;
+        let b1 = chunk.get(1).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;
+        let b2 = chunk.get(2).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;
+        let triple = (b0 << 16) | (b1 << 8) | b2;
+        result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 18) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 12) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        if chunk.len() > 1 {
+            result.push(CHARS[((triple >> 6) & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        } else {
+            result.push('=');
+        }
+        if chunk.len() > 2 {
+            result.push(CHARS[(triple & 0x3F) as usize] as char);
+        } else {
+            result.push('=');
+        }
+    }
+    result
+}

+/** Send an init message to a worker and wait for the "ready" response. */
+function workerReady(
+  worker: Worker,
+  initMsg: Record<string, unknown>,
+): Promise<void> {
+  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
+    const timeout = setTimeout(
+      () => reject(new Error("Worker init timeout")),
+      5000,
+    );
+    const handler = (e: MessageEvent) => {
+      if (e.data.type === "ready") {
+        clearTimeout(timeout);
+        worker.removeEventListener("message", handler);
+        resolve();
+      }
+    };
+    worker.addEventListener("message", handler);
+    worker.postMessage(initMsg);
+  });
+}
